Article.label: Article
Article.description: An article, such as a news article or piece of investigative report. Newspapers and magazines have articles of many different types and this is intended to cover them all.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/09/02/schema-org-support-for-bibliographic-relationships-and-periodicals/).

properties.name: article_section
properties.description: Articles may belong to one or more 'sections' in a magazine or newspaper, such as Sports, Lifestyle, etc.

properties.name: article_body
properties.description: The actual body of the article.

properties.name: backstory
properties.description: For an [[Article]], typically a [[NewsArticle]], the backstory property provides a textual summary giving a brief explanation of why and how an article was created. In a journalistic setting this coul

properties.name: word_count
properties.description: The number of words in the text of the CreativeWork such as an Article, Book, etc.

properties.name: speakable
properties.description: Indicates sections of a Web page that are particularly 'speakable' in the sense of being highlighted as being especially appropriate for text-to-speech conversion. Other sections of a page may also be

properties.name: pagination
properties.description: Any description of pages that is not separated into pageStart and pageEnd; for example, "1-6, 9, 55" or "10-12, 46-49".

properties.name: page_end
properties.description: The page on which the work ends; for example "138" or "xvi".

properties.name: page_start
properties.description: The page on which the work starts; for example "135" or "xiii".

properties.name: abridged
properties.description: Indicates whether the book is an abridged edition.

properties.name: book_edition
properties.description: The edition of the book.

properties.name: number_of_pages
properties.description: The number of pages in the book.

properties.name: isbn
properties.description: The ISBN of the book.

CreativeWork.label: CreativeWork
CreativeWork.description: The most generic kind of creative work, including books, movies, photographs, software programs, etc.

properties.name: educational_use
properties.description: The purpose of a work in the context of education; for example, 'assignment', 'group work'.

properties.name: text
properties.description: The textual content of this CreativeWork.

properties.name: alternative_headline
properties.description: A secondary title of the CreativeWork.

properties.name: learning_resource_type
properties.description: The predominant type or kind characterizing the learning resource. For example, 'presentation', 'handout'.

properties.name: temporal
properties.description:
The "temporal" property can be used in cases where more specific properties
(e.g. [[temporalCoverage]], [[dateCreated]], [[dateModified]], [[datePublished]]) are not known to be appropriate.

properties.name: date_created
properties.description: The date on which the CreativeWork was created or the item was added to a DataFeed.

properties.name: discussion_url
properties.description: A link to the page containing the comments of the CreativeWork.

properties.name: typical_age_range
properties.description: The typical expected age range, e.g. '7-9', '11-'.

properties.name: content_rating
properties.description: Official rating of a piece of content—for example, 'MPAA PG-13'.

properties.name: license
properties.description: A license document that applies to this content, typically indicated by URL.

properties.name: conditions_of_access
properties.description: Conditions that affect the availability of, or method(s) of access to, an item. Typically used for real world items such as an [[ArchiveComponent]] held by an [[ArchiveOrganization]]. This property is

properties.name: date_modified
properties.description: The date on which the CreativeWork was most recently modified or when the item's entry was modified within a DataFeed.

properties.name: is_part_of
properties.description: Indicates an item or CreativeWork that this item, or CreativeWork (in some sense), is part of.

properties.name: expires
properties.description: Date the content expires and is no longer useful or available. For example a [[VideoObject]] or [[NewsArticle]] whose availability or relevance is time-limited, a [[ClaimReview]] fact check whose publ

properties.name: citation
properties.description: A citation or reference to another creative work, such as another publication, web page, scholarly article, etc.

properties.name: material_extent
properties.description: The quantity of the materials being described or an expression of the physical space they occupy.

properties.name: word_count
properties.description: The number of words in the text of the CreativeWork such as an Article, Book, etc.

properties.name: assesses
properties.description: The item being described is intended to assess the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

properties.name: credit_text
properties.description: Text that can be used to credit person(s) and/or organization(s) associated with a published Creative Work.

properties.name: encoding_format
properties.description: Media type typically expressed using a MIME format (see [IANA site](http://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml) and [MDN reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP

properties.name: size
properties.description: A standardized size of a product or creative work, specified either through a simple textual string (for example 'XL', '32Wx34L'), a  QuantitativeValue with a unitCode, or a comprehensive and structur

properties.name: interactivity_type
properties.description: The predominant mode of learning supported by the learning resource. Acceptable values are 'active', 'expositive', or 'mixed'.

properties.name: keywords
properties.description: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: accessibility_summary
properties.description: A human-readable summary of specific accessibility features or deficiencies, consistent with the other accessibility metadata but expressing subtleties such as "short descriptions are present but long

properties.name: publishing_principles
properties.description: The publishingPrinciples property indicates (typically via [[URL]]) a document describing the editorial principles of an [[Organization]] (or individual, e.g. a [[Person]] writing a blog) that relate

properties.name: date_published
properties.description: Date of first publication or broadcast. For example the date a [[CreativeWork]] was broadcast or a [[Certification]] was issued.

properties.name: accessibility_api
properties.description: Indicates that the resource is compatible with the referenced accessibility API. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityAP

properties.name: copyright_notice
properties.description: Text of a notice appropriate for describing the copyright aspects of this Creative Work, ideally indicating the owner of the copyright for the Work.

properties.name: archived_at
properties.description: Indicates a page or other link involved in archival of a [[CreativeWork]]. In the case of [[MediaReview]], the items in a [[MediaReviewItem]] may often become inaccessible, but be archived by archival

properties.name: sd_date_published
properties.description: Indicates the date on which the current structured data was generated / published. Typically used alongside [[sdPublisher]].

properties.name: thumbnail_url
properties.description: A thumbnail image relevant to the Thing.

properties.name: acquire_license_page
properties.description: Indicates a page documenting how licenses can be purchased or otherwise acquired, for the current item.

properties.name: copyright_year
properties.description: The year during which the claimed copyright for the CreativeWork was first asserted.

properties.name: is_family_friendly
properties.description: Indicates whether this content is family friendly.

properties.name: correction
properties.description: Indicates a correction to a [[CreativeWork]], either via a [[CorrectionComment]], textually or in another document.

properties.name: is_based_on
properties.description: A resource from which this work is derived or from which it is a modification or adaptation.

properties.name: is_based_on_url
properties.description: A resource that was used in the creation of this resource. This term can be repeated for multiple sources. For example, http://example.com/great-multiplication-intro.html.

properties.name: awards
properties.description: Awards won by or for this item.

properties.name: headline
properties.description: Headline of the article.

properties.name: teaches
properties.description: The item being described is intended to help a person learn the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

properties.name: version
properties.description: The version of the CreativeWork embodied by a specified resource.

properties.name: temporal_coverage
properties.description: The temporalCoverage of a CreativeWork indicates the period that the content applies to, i.e. that it describes, either as a DateTime or as a textual string indicating a time period in [ISO 8601 time

properties.name: accessibility_feature
properties.description: Content features of the resource, such as accessible media, alternatives and supported enhancements for accessibility. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11

properties.name: creative_work_status
properties.description: The status of a creative work in terms of its stage in a lifecycle. Example terms include Incomplete, Draft, Published, Obsolete. Some organizations define a set of terms for the stages of their publi

properties.name: edit_eidr
properties.description:
An [EIDR](https://eidr.org/) (Entertainment Identifier Registry) [[identifier]] representing a specific edit / edition for a work of film or television.

For example, the motion picture known as "Ghos

properties.name: content_reference_time
properties.description: The specific time described by a creative work, for works (e.g. articles, video objects etc.) that emphasise a particular moment within an Event.

properties.name: comment_count
properties.description: The number of comments this CreativeWork (e.g. Article, Question or Answer) has received. This is most applicable to works published in Web sites with commenting system; additional comments may exist

properties.name: access_mode
properties.description: The human sensory perceptual system or cognitive faculty through which a person may process or perceive the intellectual content of a resource, not including any adaptations of the content (e.g., text

properties.name: position
properties.description: The position of an item in a series or sequence of items.

properties.name: material
properties.description: A material that something is made from, e.g. leather, wool, cotton, paper.

properties.name: pattern
properties.description: A pattern that something has, for example 'polka dot', 'striped', 'Canadian flag'. Values are typically expressed as text, although links to controlled value schemes are also supported.

properties.name: accessibility_hazard
properties.description: A characteristic of the described resource that is physiologically dangerous to some users. Related to WCAG 2.0 guideline 2.3. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/

properties.name: sd_license
properties.description: A license document that applies to this structured data, typically indicated by URL.

properties.name: usage_info
properties.description: The schema.org [[usageInfo]] property indicates further information about a [[CreativeWork]]. This property is applicable both to works that are freely available and to those that require payment or o

properties.name: award
properties.description: An award won by or for this item.

properties.name: schema_version
properties.description:
Indicates (by URL or string) a particular version of a schema used in some CreativeWork. This property was created primarily to
    indicate the use of a specific schema.org release, e.g. ```10.0``` a

properties.name: is_accessible_for_free
properties.description: A flag to signal that the item, event, or place is accessible for free.

properties.name: genre
properties.description: Genre of the creative work, broadcast channel or group.

properties.name: file_format
properties.description: Media type, typically MIME format (see [IANA site](http://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml)) of the content, e.g. application/zip of a SoftwareApplication binary. In cases where

properties.name: in_language
properties.description: The language of the content or performance or used in an action. Please use one of the language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). See also [[availableLanguage]].

properties.name: accessibility_control
properties.description: Identifies input methods that are sufficient to fully control the described resource. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibil

properties.name: abstract
properties.description: An abstract is a short description that summarizes a [[CreativeWork]].

properties.name: educational_level
properties.description: The level in terms of progression through an educational or training context. Examples of educational levels include 'beginner', 'intermediate' or 'advanced', and formal sets of level indicators.

Event.label: Event
Event.description: An event happening at a certain time and location, such as a concert, lecture, or festival. Ticketing information may be added via the [[offers]] property. Repeated events may be structured as separate Event objects.

properties.name: typical_age_range
properties.description: The typical expected age range, e.g. '7-9', '11-'.

properties.name: maximum_attendee_capacity
properties.description: The total number of individuals that may attend an event or venue.

properties.name: previous_start_date
properties.description: Used in conjunction with eventStatus for rescheduled or cancelled events. This property contains the previously scheduled start date. For rescheduled events, the startDate property should be used for

properties.name: keywords
properties.description: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The location of, for example, where an event is happening, where an organization is located, or where an action takes place.

properties.name: maximum_virtual_attendee_capacity
properties.description: The maximum virtual attendee capacity of an [[Event]] whose [[eventAttendanceMode]] is [[OnlineEventAttendanceMode]] (or the online aspects, in the case of a [[MixedEventAttendanceMode]]).

properties.name: door_time
properties.description: The time admission will commence.

properties.name: remaining_attendee_capacity
properties.description: The number of attendee places for an event that remain unallocated.

properties.name: maximum_physical_attendee_capacity
properties.description: The maximum physical attendee capacity of an [[Event]] whose [[eventAttendanceMode]] is [[OfflineEventAttendanceMode]] (or the offline aspects, in the case of a [[MixedEventAttendanceMode]]).

properties.name: end_date
properties.description: The end date and time of the item (in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

properties.name: start_date
properties.description: The start date and time of the item (in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

properties.name: is_accessible_for_free
properties.description: A flag to signal that the item, event, or place is accessible for free.

properties.name: in_language
properties.description: The language of the content or performance or used in an action. Please use one of the language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). See also [[availableLanguage]].

Organization.label: Organization
Organization.description: An organization such as a school, NGO, corporation, club, etc.

properties.name: dissolution_date
properties.description: The date that this organization was dissolved.

properties.name: telephone
properties.description: The telephone number.

properties.name: founding_date
properties.description: The date that this organization was founded.

properties.name: duns
properties.description: The Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number for identifying an organization or business person.

properties.name: knows_about

properties.name: naics
properties.description: The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for a particular organization or business person.

properties.name: address
properties.description: Physical address of the item.

properties.name: logo
properties.description: An associated logo.

properties.name: global_location_number
properties.description: The [Global Location Number](http://www.gs1.org/gln) (GLN, sometimes also referred to as International Location Number or ILN) of the respective organization, person, or place. The GLN is a 13-digit n

properties.description: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: tax_id
properties.description: The Tax / Fiscal ID of the organization or person, e.g. the TIN in the US or the CIF/NIF in Spain.

properties.name: publishing_principles
properties.description: The publishingPrinciples property indicates (typically via [[URL]]) a document describing the editorial principles of an [[Organization]] (or individual, e.g. a [[Person]] writing a blog) that relate

properties.name: corrections_policy
properties.description: For an [[Organization]] (e.g. [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a statement describing (in news media, the newsroom’s) disclosure and correction policy for errors.

properties.name: legal_name
properties.description: The official name of the organization, e.g. the registered company name.

properties.name: unnamed_sources_policy
properties.description: For an [[Organization]] (typically a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a statement about policy on use of unnamed sources and the decision process required.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The location of, for example, where an event is happening, where an organization is located, or where an action takes place.

properties.name: email
properties.description: Email address.

properties.name: knows_language
properties.description: Of a [[Person]], and less typically of an [[Organization]], to indicate a known language. We do not distinguish skill levels or reading/writing/speaking/signing here. Use language codes from the [IETF

properties.name: vat_id
properties.description: The value-added Tax ID of the organization or person with national prefix (for example IT123456789). Can also be described as [[iso6523Code]] with proper prefix.

properties.name: ethics_policy
properties.description: Statement about ethics policy, e.g. of a [[NewsMediaOrganization]] regarding journalistic and publishing practices, or of a [[Restaurant]], a page describing food source policies. In the case of a [[N

properties.name: awards
properties.description: Awards won by or for this item.

properties.name: has_gs1_digital_link
properties.description: The GS1 digital link associated with the object. This URL should conform to the particular requirements of digital links. The link should o

properties.name: accepted_payment_method
properties.description: The payment method(s) that are accepted in general by an organization, or for some specific demand or offer.

properties.name: actionable_feedback_policy
properties.description: For a [[NewsMediaOrganization]] or other news-related [[Organization]], a statement about public engagement activities (for news media, the newsroom’s), including involving the public - digitally or o

properties.name: slogan
properties.description: A slogan or motto associated with the item.

properties.name: award
properties.description: An award won by or for this item.

properties.name: skills
properties.description: A statement of knowledge, skill, ability, task or any other assertion expressing a competency that is either claimed by a person, an organization or desired or required to fulfill a role or to work in

properties.name: fax_number
properties.description: The fax number.

properties.name: isic_v4
properties.description: The International Standard of Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities (ISIC), Revision 4 code for a particular organization, business person, or place.

properties.name: diversity_policy
properties.description: Statement on diversity policy by an [[Organization]] e.g. a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]. For a [[NewsMediaOrganization]], a statement describing the newsroom’s diversity policy on both staffing and sour

properties.name: area_served
properties.description: The geographic area where a service or offered item is provided.

properties.description: For an [[Organization]] (often but not necessarily a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a description of organizational ownership structure; funding and grants. In a news/media setting, this is with particul

properties.name: family_name
properties.description: Family name. In the U.S., the last name of a Person.

properties.name: job_title

properties.name: birth_date

properties.name: public_access
properties.description: A flag to signal that the [[Place]] is open to public visitors.  If this property is omitted there is no assumed default boolean value.

relations.description: An actor (individual or a group), e.g. in TV, radio, movie, video games etc., or in an event. Actors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.
